title: Colly Collectors, Callback Order, Limits, Storage, and Adoption Boundaries · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for colly collectors, callback order, limits, storage, and adoption boundaries and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit the…
objectives: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.
Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Colly Lifecycle · changed case · Quiz Colly Lifecycle

quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 2911 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to map Colly lifecycle, policy, callbacks, storage, and differentials without losing owned invariants; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only.
